commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive runtimePackages/Codex launcher behavior; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff uses existing packaged Codex/runtime artifacts and repo scripts, though the PATH/HOME changes remain compatibility-sensitive. (sha=2c015b816b5de879f9a4f452ce67a9d5f29df572)
commands.review_summary:
The branch splits generic runtime tool PATH handling from the Codex app-server adapter, removes activation-time Codex native-profile linking, adds a Nix-managed Codex launcher wrapper, and adds runtime-path/activation checks and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. with source/proof rather than a rerun in this read-only review: current main does not render runtimePackages into tools.exec.pathPrepend and the PR body shows native Codex command/exec failing before and resolving gog after the launcher.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive runtimePackages/Codex launcher behavior; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

OpenClaw-managed Codex command execution could not see Nix runtime tools such as `gog`.

That is narrower than “OpenClaw cannot run tools.” Generic OpenClaw command execution and native Codex command execution are different paths. Upstream OpenClaw gives the Codex app-server a per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, but native Codex still inherits a process `HOME` unless the app-server command adapts it. In nix-openclaw, `runtimePackages` were available to the gateway/runtime tool path but not reliably visible inside native Codex `command/exec`, so `gog` could be installed by Nix and still fail inside an OpenClaw Codex turn.

## Fix

This PR separates the two concepts and names the boundary in code and docs:

- `runtime-tools.nix` owns generic OpenClaw command tools: gateway `PATH` and generated upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- `codex-app-server.nix` owns only the Nix adapter for the packaged upstream `codex` runtime plugin.
- The Nix Codex launcher runs only for local stdio app-server launches selected by nix-openclaw. It reads OpenClaw-provided `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, links `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin` to the Nix runtime profile, prepends that bin dir, then execs upstream Codex.
- Home Manager activation no longer tries to predict Codex agent directories or create Codex native-home profiles.

The Nix launcher is not offered when the user provides `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`, a configured or inherited `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`, or `appServer.transport = "websocket"`.

## Non-Goals

This PR does not add a public knob, does not add a host PATH fallback list, does not export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`, and does not enable the Codex plugin from `runtimePackages` alone.

## Upstream Contract Pointers

The code comments point future maintainers at the upstream contracts that matter:

- `docs/tools/exec.md`, `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`, and `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` for OpenClaw command PATH handling.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts`, and `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md` for Codex `CODEX_HOME`, app-server command precedence, stdio vs WebSocket transport, and env overrides.

## Proof Of The Actual Failure/Fix

Current head:

```text
2c015b816b5de879f9a4f452ce67a9d5f29df572
```

Command:

```bash
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
nix log /nix/store/3p2db3qsz1q3494xfshshsf9wh5ni3bq-openclaw-runtime-path-1.drv | sed -n '/openclaw-runtime-path proof:/,$p'
```

Output:

```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend resolves /nix/store/c287nl219lwgfzfmld92v75r094l47qz-hello-2.12.2/bin/hello
- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages alone do not configure the Codex app-server launcher
- selecting the Nix-packaged Codex runtime plugin installs the native-home app-server launcher
- a user appServer.command keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- a user OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- inherited OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- Codex websocket transport keeps the local stdio launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- before Nix-managed Codex launcher: exitCode=127
HOME=/homeless-shelter
PATH=/homeless-shelter/.nix-profile/bin:/run/current-system/sw/bin:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
OPENCLAW_GOG_MISSING
- after Nix-managed Codex launcher: exitCode=0
HOME=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-65943-2020870885/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-zuDH4w/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home
PATH=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-65943-2020870885/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-zuDH4w/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin:/run/current-system/sw/bin:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-65943-2020870885/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-zuDH4w/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog
v0.15.0 (e0338d5 2026-05-05T05:50:19Z)
- Nix Codex launcher links /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-65943-2020870885/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-zuDH4w/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin -> /nix/store/n240bwbrsnzckviz165x4vxw7w16iyjk-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
- native Codex app-server resolves /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-65943-2020870885/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-zuDH4w/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog from HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home
```

That is the actual broken path: native Codex app-server JSON-RPC `command/exec`, with `HOME`, `PATH`, `command -v gog`, version output, and exit code before and after the Nix-managed launcher.

## Local Verification

These passed locally on the current patch:

```bash
git diff --check
node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path.drvPath
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-path.drvPath
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation
OPENCLAW_HM_ACTIVATION_PACKAGE="$PWD/result-platform-activation" scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation
```

The activation tests prove the non-Codex boundary: `runtimePackages` alone render gateway/config command paths but do not create Codex profile state or Codex app-server env.

## CI Coverage

The GitHub CI workflow runs the relevant checks on this PR:

- Linux supported surface builds `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation`.
- macOS supported surface builds `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation`.
- macOS CI also runs `scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh` against the built `platform-activation` out-link.
